message:
fix(raptorq): saturate repair_count against k_prime overflow + wolfram test token constraints + manifest_parse fuzz target

Three independent improvements pulled in together.

## 1. fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs: u32 overflow guard on repair_count (br-gh7gt)

Both `RaptorQEncoder::encode_all()` and
`RaptorQEncoder::into_encode_all()` build a Vec of
`(symbol_id, payload)` pairs where `symbol_id = k_prime + i` for i in
`0..repair_count`. Both `k_prime` and `repair_count` are u32 values
that come from RFC 6330 extended-source-block arithmetic; their sum
normally stays well under u32::MAX, but the crate offers no enforced
upper bound on `repair_symbols`. A config that returned an absurdly
large repair_count would wrap the u32 add in release and panic in
debug — surprising for a library that otherwise uses saturating /
checked arithmetic on symbol counts.

Fix: cap repair_count at `u32::MAX - k_prime` before the loop. When
the caller's repair request exceeds that headroom, we silently emit
the maximum safe count instead of panicking. Matches the rest of the
crate's "don't blow up on pathological-but-legal inputs" discipline.

    let repair_count = self.repair_symbols().min(u32::MAX - k_prime);
    // ... later:
    for i in 0..repair_count {
        let symbol_id = k_prime + i;   // always < u32::MAX now
        ...
    }

Same change mirrored in `into_encode_all()` with comment linking back
to the in-place `encode_all()` guard so future readers see both
copies are maintained together.

Tracks flywheel_connectors-gh7gt.

## 2. connectors/wolfram/src/connector.rs: test tokens need constraints

The Wolfram|Alpha invoke path now asserts `constraints.resource_allow`
contains the target resource (or `*`) before any outbound HTTP call —
that's part of the br-k5rux fail-closed hardening that landed earlier.
The existing test fixtures in `mod tests` built CapabilityTokens via
`CapabilityTokenBuilder::new()…sign()` with no constraints CBOR, so
the happy-path tests started failing after the resource gate was
added.

Fix: build a `CapabilityConstraints { resource_allow: vec!["*"], .. }`
in the test helper `generate_token()`, serialize it with
`ciborium::into_writer`, and pass the bytes to
`.constraints_cbor(&constraints_cbor)` on the builder. The `"*"`
allow-list matches the existing fixtures' permissive intent — tests
that specifically exercise the allow-list are separate and continue
to use narrower constraints.

New imports: `ciborium::into_writer`, plus `CapabilityConstraints`
added to the existing `fcp_core` import list.

## 3. fuzz: new fuzz_manifest_parse target + 6 seed corpora

Add `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_manifest_parse.rs` (245 lines) targeting
the connector manifest parser. Registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml` under
the standard `[[bin]]` shape (test = false, doc = false, bench = false
— matches the other manifest fuzz targets in this crate).

Seed corpus (`fuzz/corpus/fuzz_manifest_parse/`, 6 fixtures):
  - truncated_cbor.json                 — malformed payload boundary
  - reordered_fields.json               — canonical-order drift
  - duplicate_keys.json                 — CBOR map-key collision
  - utf8_edge_connector_names.json      — surrogate / BOM / RTL fixtures
  - malicious_signature_blobs.json      — oversized signature arrays
  - deeply_nested_capability_trees.json — recursion-depth attack

Each seed is small (66-98 bytes) so libfuzzer can mutate aggressively
from a known-interesting starting point instead of spending the first
millions of iterations rediscovering the alphabet.

## Beads

Minor metadata only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1244,6 +1244,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","title":"fcp.airtable: Full CRUD operations on records","description":"# fcp.airtable: Full CRUD Operations on Records

## Goal
Implement record CRUD with batching, pagination, and strict safety controls.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.records.list` (pagination, filter/sort)
- `airtable.records.get`
- `airtable.records.create` / `airtable.records.create_batch`
- `airtable.records.update` / `airtable.records.update_batch`
- `airtable.records.upsert`
- `airtable.records.delete` / `airtable.records.delete_batch` (Dangerous)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write vs delete are separate capabilities
- Rate limiting and backpressure are mechanical.
- Pagination:
  - support continuation tokens
  - bound page sizes
- Batch operations:
  - handle partial failures deterministically
  - stable error mapping per record
- Idempotency / retries:
  - upsert is preferred for retry-safe workflows
  - deletes and schema writes must be treated as Dangerous (policy + receipts)

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - pagination edge cases
  - batch partial failure semantics
  - upsert conflict handling
  - redaction: never log record bodies by default

## Acceptance Criteria
- CRUD operations behave deterministically under retries.
- Dangerous operations are properly gated and audited.
","notes":"In-progress implementation slice completed: added Airtable batch update, upsert, and batch delete surfaces across client/connector/manifest/tests. Package-level verification passed via rch for fmt/check/test/clippy.

Current state after this pass:
- new operations: airtable.update_records, airtable.upsert_records, airtable.delete_records
- new client helpers and response types added
- connector validation now enforces batch size and merge-field requirements for the new paths
- integration coverage added for client + connector invoke flows

Still pending for full bead closure:
- assess whether single-record CRUD should also resolve table refs through schema for stricter consistency
- consider deterministic partial-failure mapping / richer batch error surfaces if Airtable returns mixed mutation outcomes
- complete workspace-wide verification","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:33.751522695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.494549Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.494536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1054,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"ChartreuseLake","text":"Implemented Airtable batch update/upsert/delete plus tightened single-record and batch-create connector paths to resolve table refs through cached schema and validate fields payload shape. Added connector coverage for table-name resolution on create/create_batch/delete; package fmt/check/test/clippy are passing.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:23:28Z"},{"id":1055,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"ChartreuseLake","text":"Status after verification: keeping this bead IN_PROGRESS rather than closing it yet. The CRUD surface is now materially fuller and more consistent, but the acceptance text still explicitly calls out deterministic batch partial-failure semantics / stable per-record error mapping. Package-level fmt/check/test/clippy are green; workspace clippy remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing failures in connectors/box and connectors/asana.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:27:16Z"},{"id":1305,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented records.rs module: Record CRUD with builder pattern ListRecordsParams, batch operations (max 10), pagination (max 100), RecordValidator. 145 tests, all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g37n0","title":"fcp-slack: ok=true responses can panic on missing payload data","description":"## Finding
`SlackApiResponse<T>` stores the flattened success payload as `Option<T>` ([connectors/slack/src/types.rs:123-129](connectors/slack/src/types.rs:123)). `check_response()` only checks `ok` ([connectors/slack/src/client.rs:447-458](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:447)). Multiple client paths then call `resp.data.expect("ok response has data")` ([connectors/slack/src/client.rs:162](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:162), [209](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:209), [228](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:228), [238](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:238), [257](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:257), [270](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:270), [282](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:282), [302](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:302), [312](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:312)).

A malformed or partial `ok:true` body therefore becomes a process panic instead of an `FcpError`/`SlackError`. The crate already documents the `None` case in tests (`slack_api_response_error_has_no_data`), so this is not theoretical parser behavior.

## Repro shape
Return `{"ok":true}` or `{"ok":true,"channel":"C1"}` from `chat.postMessage`. `check_response()` accepts it and the subsequent `.expect(...)` aborts the connector.

## Recommendation
Replace each `.expect("ok response has data")` with an error-mapped `ok_or_else(...)` path that preserves the operation context and treats malformed success envelopes as API/protocol errors, not panics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:56:43.789357Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29.875248Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29.872787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2811,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g37n0","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in 2dd92d08. Root cause: 9 client paths called .expect("ok response has data") on a flattened Option<T> payload — a partial {"ok":true} envelope therefore panicked the connector instead of surfacing as an error. Fix: added Self::expect_data(data, method) helper that maps None to a terminal SlackError::Api { ok: true, error: "Slack API method \`X\` returned ok=true with no payload", .. }, and migrated all 9 call sites through it. The ok:true discriminator preserves the partial-envelope case as distinct from classic ok=false errors. Regression test ok_true_with_missing_payload_is_mapped_to_api_error_not_panic asserts the method name and 'ok=true' phrase appear in the error, and that to_fcp_error() maps to FcpError::External. Verified: 210 lib tests + 51/52 integration tests pass (the one pre-existing failure list_channels_happy_path is an unrelated URL-encoding bug confirmed by bisect).","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","title":"[FCP3/P3.2] Define canonical FCPS durable object schemas for runtime truth","description":"Background: a mesh first platform needs durable forms for the facts that survive process death and justify later decisions. Receipts, checkpoints, lease state, revocations, connector runtime state, and operator explanations cannot remain host local conventions.

Scope: define canonical FCPS object schemas for durable state, including connector identity and state, receipts, checkpoints, lease and fencing records, revocation records, and explanation bundles. Design them for replay, repair, inspection, and failover rather than as opaque storage details. Provide deterministic object examples, snapshot formats, explanation bundle shapes, schema unit tests, replay integration tests, and detailed inspection logs that later conformance and end to end harnesses can diff and diagnose.

Acceptance: the platform has a durable object vocabulary for the facts it must remember, those facts no longer depend on one host memory image to exist, and the schemas are concrete enough for fixture driven validation, replay, operator inspection, and regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.315492Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065699Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065467Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fcps","mesh-first","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2m2hl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.219178Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.654535Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2081,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Published FCPS durable object schemas at docs/FCP3_FCPS_Durable_Object_Schemas.md. Defines 7 object types: OperationReceipt (immutable execution proof), Checkpoint (state snapshot with lineage), Lease (exclusive ownership with fencing), Revocation (capability/session invalidation), ConnectorIdentity (deployment trust record), ExplanationBundle (human-readable decision justification), ConnectorState (durable runtime state). Common FcpsDurableObject header with schema versioning, content-addressing, and signatures. Includes fixtures, evolution rules, and inspection CLI commands.","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:08Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-gh7gt","title":"fcp-raptorq: encode_all/into_encode_all ESI arithmetic uses wrapping add, not checked_add","description":"In `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs:141-144` (`encode_all`) and `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs:162-166` (`into_encode_all`), the repair symbol ESI is computed as `k_prime + i` where both are u32. In debug builds, overflow panics; in release, it wraps into the source-symbol ESI range and silently collides with existing source symbols.

**Bounded in practice.** `config.repair_symbols(source_symbols)` saturates to `u32::MAX`, but `source_symbols` comes from `payload_len.div_ceil(symbol_size)` where `payload_len ≤ config.max_object_size` (typically 64 MiB) and `symbol_size >= 1`. So `source_symbols ≤ 64 MiB`, well under u32::MAX. The current config bounds keep the arithmetic safe.

**Brittle.** The rest of the crate uses `saturating_add` / `checked_add` consistently (see `config.rs:155`, `decode.rs:585`, `decode.rs:598`). `encode.rs:142` and `encode.rs:164` are the only sites that use raw `+` on user-derived-ish arithmetic with no bound check.

**Suggested fix**: `let esi = k_prime.checked_add(i).ok_or(EncodeError::SymbolCountOverflow)?;` (or saturating_add). Mirror the `decode.rs:585` pattern.

Severity: LOW. Not currently exploitable; filed as a defensive-depth bead to keep the saturating/checked arithmetic discipline uniform across the crate.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:51.293708Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:51.293708Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","title":"fcp.pinecone: Pinecone Vector Database Connector","description":"# fcp.pinecone - Pinecone Vector Database Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant connector for Pinecone (managed vector DB) supporting vector storage and semantic retrieval under strict zone isolation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `knowledge`

## Zone model
Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the configured Pinecone endpoint(s) and port(s) required by declared operations.

### Credential injection
- API key via `CredentialId`.
- Never persist or log keys.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `pinecone.index.manage`
- `pinecone.vectors.read`
- `pinecone.vectors.write`
- `pinecone.search.query`

## Operations (initial)
- index: create/describe/list/delete
- vectors: upsert/fetch/delete/query

## Tests
- Mock-only unit tests with wiremock.
- E2E compliance run validates default deny + NetworkConstraints + receipts for writes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner.
- Deterministic tests (no live Pinecone calls).

d_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":366,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Pinecone connector implemented: 7 files (Cargo.toml, lib.rs, main.rs, error.rs, types.rs, client.rs, connector.rs). 7 operations (list_indexes, describe_index, describe_index_stats, query, fetch, upsert, delete). Dual-plane architecture (control + data). Api-Key header auth. 19 tests passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:07:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif.1","title":"fcp.pinecone: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.pinecone: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Pinecone connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`storage`, `knowledge`)
- operations + schema IDs
- capability families + per-operation caps
- NetworkConstraints allowlist bound to configured endpoint

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad manifest examples exist for static compliance.

Changes:
- client.rs: Added create_index() and delete_index() methods with DELETE HTTP helper
- connector.rs: Added invoke_create_index/invoke_delete_index handlers, updated dispatch + introspect (7→9 ops)
- manifest.toml: Added pinecone.create_index + pinecone.delete_index with schemas, network constraints, rate limits
- Interface hash regenerated via fcp manifest fix
- tests: 6 new tests (2 client unit, 3 integration + 1 cap rejection) — all 25 integration tests pass
- Validation: cargo check + clippy + fmt + test all clean","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:57:29Z"}]}

@@ -136,6 +136,13 @@ test = false

+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_manifest_parse"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_manifest_parse.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_mesh_gossip_summary"
 path = "fuzz_targets/mesh_gossip_summary.rs"
